parsed.simulated_user_message: I feel like I'm drowning—my parents are moving in, my sister and I can't even agree on how to help, and I keep getting pulled back into this huge work project that feels impossible to finish. Every night I lie awake, thinking about the guilt of not being able to fix everything. I miss my friends and the quiet time we used to share.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here as a supportive friend, and it sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed by all the changes and responsibilities in your life right now. Given your intent to express emotion, I'll start by acknowledging the weight of those feelings. My approach will be to use complex reflection to help you explore your emotions and thoughts. I've taken note of your past experiences, such as the loss of a friend and argument with your sister, which might be contributing to your current emotional state. My first response will be to acknowledge the sense of drowning you mentioned, and how that must feel like a huge burden.

evidence_used.content: loss of a friend, argument with sister

parsed.final_response: You're feeling like you're drowning in all these responsibilities and changes, and it's no wonder - your parents moving in, disagreements with your sister, and this huge work project are all big things to handle. It sounds like the weight of not being able to fix everything is really getting to you, especially at night when everything feels more overwhelming. I can imagine how much you miss the quiet time and connection with your friends. Yeah, it's a lot to carry on your own.
